summary: Stripe checkout integration tested end-to-end. Backend: 28/28 pytest cases pass (19 regression + 9 new Stripe tests) — real checkout.stripe.com URLs returned, server-side pricing verified (Elden Ring 1x -> 3499 cents, 2x -> 6998 cents, multi-item sum verified), client-supplied 'price' field ignored, 400 for empty/invalid cart, 404 for non-existent session, payment_transactions doc created with status/payment_status='initiated' before redirect, and no order fabricated while payment_status='unpaid'. Frontend: /checkout form submits and triggers POST /api/checkout/session which returns a stripe.com URL; browser actually navigated to checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... . /checkout/success shows data-testid='status-checking' when session_id is present and 'status-failed' when missing.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _compute_cart: silently skips invalid product_ids (continue) — the aggregate 400 check catches all-invalid carts, but a partially invalid cart will still succeed with a smaller total. Acceptable but consider returning 400 with the offending id list for better UX.
server.py get_checkout_status: instantiates a fresh StripeCheckout on every poll (2s interval frontend). Fine functionally but could be hoisted to a module-level instance for efficiency.

server.py _fulfill_order: idempotency guard reads txn['order_created']; there is a very small race window between the read and the update_one({session_id}, {order_created: True}). If webhook and status-poll fire simultaneously (rare) two orders could be created. Consider using a conditional update ({'order_created': {'$ne': True}}) or a unique index on session_id in orders collection.
server.py stripe_webhook: no persistence of Stripe-Signature verification result beyond exception; emergentintegrations handles this, but consider logging event.event_type for audit trail.
Checkout.jsx: on failure it clears submitting flag but a fresh submit could race with an in-flight request — button is disabled during submitting so acceptable.
CheckoutSuccess.jsx: MAX_ATTEMPTS=8 * 2s = 16s window; if Stripe redirects back before payment finalizes it could show 'timeout'. Consider surfacing a manual 'refresh status' button after timeout.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event(startup/shutdown) — migrate to lifespan handlers (noted in prev iteration, still not fixed).
seed_data_creation: Created several payment_transactions with 'TEST_' customer_name prefix via the pytest suite. These remain in the payment_transactions collection in 'initiated'/'unpaid' state (no orders created since payments weren't completed). No cleanup needed for MVP.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stripe test key is sk_test_emergent (Emergent test proxy) — real checkout.stripe.com URLs are generated. To verify server-side pricing without completing a hosted payment: POST /api/checkout/session with items+customer info+origin_url, then GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} and assert amount_total (in cents) matches sum(DB price * quantity)*100. Elden Ring seed product ($34.99) is stable and used as canonical amount check (3499 cents). payment_transactions collection stores each session; verify order_created guard in _fulfill_order remains in place. Frontend adds items via CartContext (localStorage 'nn_cart'); tests can seed cart directly via localStorage to bypass the 3D catalog page.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration. All Stripe integration criteria satisfied: (1) real Stripe URL returned, (2) server-side amount authoritative (client 'price' field on CheckoutItem is ignored — Pydantic model only declares product_id + quantity), (3) payment_transactions record persisted before redirect (proven by GET /checkout/status returning 200 for freshly created sessions), (4) no order created while payment_status='unpaid' (verified by polling status 3 times and checking /admin/orders), (5) empty cart returns 400, invalid ids return 400, non-existent session returns 404, (6) frontend redirect to checkout.stripe.com confirmed, (7) status-checking UI visible with session_id, status-failed without.
